openQuestions.question: Zone is absent from the 18-surface map — need explicit build tickets (or explicit cuts) for all 12+ pages.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Cut tickets from this spec's 9-lens composition; nothing is silently dropped.

openQuestions.question: Meeting Brief has no home anywhere in the kit.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: T1 carry-over: port MeetingBriefPage unchanged as a cockpit/WorkspaceHome tab; design pass later.

openQuestions.question: Onboard-as-Client handoff is dead code at HEAD (no #new-workspace consumer) — wire or cut?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Wire: prospect report → New-workspace form with URL prefill (small, ui-only).

openQuestions.question: Requests: how do 4 sub-tools map onto the prototype's single feed?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Hybrid: prototype feed for signals + promote flow; retain RequestManager (status machine, priorities, bulk) as an 'All requests' view behind it.

openQuestions.question: Where do per-workspace outcome tabs (scorecard/actions/learnings/playbooks/coverage/RecordPublishedWork) live? 'Cockpit/Insights Engine' is undemonstrated.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep OutcomeDashboard mounted behind the book table's 'Cockpit →' link until an Insights-Engine rehome is specced.

openQuestions.question: Diagnostics: keep the registry nav entry + reports list, or prototype drill-in-only?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep nav entry and add a list lens — without it old reports become unreachable after insight resolution.

openQuestions.question: Workspace-settings depth: full 7-tab parity vs quick-win + legacy-route fallback?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Full parity — all 14 unhomed capabilities are ui-only ports, so the fallback hybrid buys little.

openQuestions.question: Client onboarding questionnaire/wizard rehome.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Owned by D4 C-phase (C2 re-skin); this zone only preserves the admin enable/reset controls.

openQuestions.question: Sign-off on the 7 prototype-only features (see kitNewFeatures).
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Adopt the 4 ui-only/S ones; defer tz-locale + stage-backing-move; adopt promote-to-signal flagged.

openQuestions.question: Outcomes value framing: replace or augment win-rate/scored-30d/coverage/attention columns?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Additive: add value fields (sn-global-ops-3), keep the HEAD columns — no silent field loss.

openQuestions.question: Strategy-inputs relocation (Strategy → workspace settings) single-source-of-truth.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Relocate with a redirect note; confirm with the Strategy surface owner before removing StrategySettings.

verdicts.evidence: server/routes/workspaces.ts + server/workspaces.ts: `grep -in archiv` → 0 hits in both. listWorkspaces() is `SELECT * FROM workspaces` with no status/deleted/archived filter (server/workspaces.ts:327 listAll stmt, :483-486 listWorkspaces body). No archived_at migration exists (server/db/migrations/ ends at 175; none reference workspace archive). No PATCH archive/unarchive route among workspaces.ts endpoints. NOTE the `action_outcomes_archive` twin (server/db/archive-twin.ts) is OUTCOME archival, unrelated to workspace archival — a naming-drift trap the gatherer correctly did not fall into. Effort S is plausible but note the DB-column+mapper lockstep: adding archived_at needs migration + WorkspaceRow interface + rowToWorkspace mapper + list-filter change + PATCH route + Zod — leans S/M, not trivially S.
verdicts.correction: existsToday=no stands. Flag effort as S-bordering-M (touches mapper + serialization lockstep, not just an endpoint).

verdicts.evidence: shared/types/workspace.ts + server/routes/workspaces.ts + server/workspaces.ts: `grep -in 'timezone|locale|time_zone'` → 0 hits. The parenthetical premise ('reporting cadence already exists') is TRUE: Workspace has autoReportFrequency?: 'weekly' | 'monthly' (shared/types/workspace.ts:363, WorkspaceRow :502) and server/monthly-report.ts + cron drive it — but no timezone/locale field backs report timestamps. existsToday=no is correct.

server/routes/outcomes.ts:223-236 — the overview route pushes EXACTLY {workspaceId, workspaceName, winRate, trend, activeActions, scoredLast30d, topWin, attentionNeeded, attentionReason, coverage}; no value-$/mo, no clicks aggregate. WorkspaceOutcomeOverview (shared/types/outcome-tracking.ts:498-514) matches. getWinsWithValueByWorkspace (server/outcome-tracking.ts:83) is verified reusable for the value side: pure SELECT (no snapshot write on read — passes the computeROI hot-read hazard) and ALREADY applies `ta.attribution != 'not_acted_on'` (line ~93), so the attribution-honesty exclusion the claim demands is present. BUT it returns per-action win rows, not a per-workspace value-$/mo aggregate — a new aggregate query/projection is genuinely net-new. 'partial' + effort M is accurate.

server/routes/requests.ts routes are CRUD-only: POST /api/requests, /batch, PATCH /bulk, GET (list/detail), PATCH /:id, POST /:id/notes, DELETE /:id, POST /:id/notes-with-files (lines 93-282). `grep -in 'promote|signal|insight'` on requests.ts → 0 hits. No request→insight bridge under any synonym across server/ (checked requestToInsight/promoteRequest/createInsightFrom/insightFromRequest — 0). The lexicon entries for promote/promotable are wordClass=proposed (shared/types/lexicon.ts, def refs 'Mockup requests.js/store.js') — mockup-only, not implemented. existsToday=no + effort M stand.

The diagnostics→recommendation MAPPING already exists and the gatherer missed it. appendDiagnosticRecommendations (server/domains/recommendations/maintenance-producers.ts:167-217) reads completed diagnostic reports' remediationActions and pushes them as recommendations via RecSource.diagnostic(reportId, actionIdx, actionTitle) (encoder at server/domains/recommendations/rules.ts:181-182; wired into the batch rec-gen cycle at generation-service.ts:271 'Diagnostic remediation recommendations'). So 'convert a remediation plan into a recommendation/backing-move row' is NOT net-new machinery — the source type, opportunity scoring, and rec shape are built. What is genuinely absent: (a) an ON-DEMAND 'stage a SPECIFIC remediation action as a backing move' trigger (today it's automated, batch-only, capped top-3-reports × top-5-actions — no HTTP endpoint to promote one chosen action), and (b) the report-export projection (server/routes/diagnostics.ts is 4 read-only GETs; no export/PDF route anywhere).
verdicts.correction: Downgrade from a full net-new build. Reuse RecSource.diagnostic + appendDiagnosticRecommendations logic; the real net-new work is (a) a single-action on-demand stage endpoint reusing that mapping, and (b) the diagnostic-report export projection. Effort M is defensible only because of (b); the 'backing move' half is closer to S (adapt existing producer). Correct existsToday from 'no' to 'partial'.
